pattern: XOR-ing along an edge flips both endpoints; since any even number of flips can be achieved, greedily XOR pairs of nodes where doing so increases the sum, ensuring an even total number of XOR operations.
hints: Applying XOR k along an edge changes both endpoints simultaneously; any subset of nodes can be XORed with k as long as the subset has even size (due to the pairing requirement).
Compute the gain of XORing each node: gain[i] = (nums[i] XOR k) - nums[i]; greedily apply XOR to nodes with positive gain.
If the count of positive-gain nodes is odd, either drop the smallest positive gain or absorb the least-negative gain to maintain even count; return sum(nums) + max total gain.

edge_inputs.description: XOR makes things worse — do nothing
one node benefits from XOR, one does not
two nodes both max value with k=1
odd number of positive-gain nodes — must drop one
k XOR nums[i] == nums[i] when k=0 edge case — but k>=1 per constraints; test k=1 with all-zero nums

stress.description: random tree with nodes 0..n-1 and random values, random k
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = size
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 10**9) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, 10**9)
    edges = []
    for i in range(1, n):
        u = rng.randint(0, i - 1)
        edges.append([u, i])
    return [nums, k, edges]
